evaluation.manager_summary: High-urgency alcohol withdrawal crisis handled with excellent triage instincts. Agent correctly identified severe withdrawal symptoms (shaking, vomiting, auditory hallucinations), coordinated same-day transport to detox, and provided clear safety instructions. Compliance concern: agent explained medical mechanisms of withdrawal rather than deferring clinical language, though the urgency response itself was appropriate.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: Agent immediately acknowledged Katherine's fear, used her name, matched the emotional urgency of the situation, and explicitly validated her decision to call. The tone was warm, present, and human throughout—nothing transactional or scripted.

Agent captured carrier, member ID, and group number efficiently in context of an urgent situation. Insurance was framed as a logistical step, not a barrier. Only small omission: no explicit hedge about pending verification.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: In urgent situations, still consider a brief hedge like 'our team will verify coverage when you arrive' to stay compliant.

dimension_scores.ai_rationale: This was an urgent medical crisis requiring immediate triage and transport, not a standard intake screening. Agent correctly prioritized safety assessment over comprehensive substance use history.

Agent immediately caught multiple high-urgency signals (shaking, vomiting, auditory hallucinations), correctly assessed this as a same-day medical emergency requiring supervised detox, and escalated appropriately with transport coordination and safety checks.
Caller is the patient herself seeking help for her own withdrawal symptoms.
No objections, hesitation, or resistance expressed. Caller was cooperative and urgent throughout, following all agent direction.

Agent provided crystal-clear next steps: call Uber immediately, go to specific address (4800 North Lakeview Drive), ask for Deborah at front desk, and stated that the detox team would be expecting her. Timing (today, after this call) and ownership fully established.

Multiple scope overreaches into clinical/medical territory. Agent stated that withdrawal symptoms 'can be medically serious,' explained auditory hallucinations as 'an early sign of alcohol withdrawal,' and stated 'alcohol withdrawal at this stage can escalate'—all clinical characterizations that should be deferred to medical staff. While the urgency was real and triage was appropriate, the agent crossed into explaining medical mechanisms rather than simply flagging symptoms and escalating.
dimension_scores.coaching_note: In crisis triage, flag symptoms urgently without explaining the medical mechanism. Example: 'What you're describing needs immediate medical evaluation' rather than 'those are withdrawal symptoms that can escalate.'

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Katherine, I hear you and I'm glad you called.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Good. Relief is the right feeling. You did the right thing calling. We'll take it from here.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: That's right. How are you feeling right now, in this moment?

evidence_anchors.speaker: caller
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Yes, Meridian Care. Member ID BP12856650, group GRP-769687.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I have that. I'm putting a note in right now with your name, insurance, and that you're coming in today.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: those are withdrawal symptoms and they can be medically serious. I want to make sure you're safe. Are you alone right now?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: that's an early sign of alcohol withdrawal that we take very seriously. I want to be direct with you: alcohol withdrawal at this stage can escalate and it needs medical supervision.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: call us back if anything gets worse before you arrive — new symptoms, chest pain, confusion.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: call the Uber right after we hang up. Our address is 4800 North Lakeview Drive. When you arrive, tell the front desk your name and that you spoke with Deborah this morning and that this is urgent.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Our detox team will be expecting you.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: Can you repeat the address back to me?

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: those are withdrawal symptoms and they can be medically serious.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: that's an early sign of alcohol withdrawal that we take very seriously. alcohol withdrawal at this stage can escalate and it needs medical supervision.

evidence_anchors.speaker: agent
evidence_anchors.text_snippet: I'd actually recommend not driving given what you're describing — your coordination may be affected.
